checks.title: Root account MFA enabled
checks.reason: Root account MFA is NOT enabled
checks.remediation: Enable MFA on the root account: AWS Console → IAM → Dashboard → Activate MFA on root account. Use a hardware MFA device for maximum security.

checks.title: No root account access keys
checks.remediation: Delete root account access keys: AWS Console → IAM → Security Credentials → Delete access keys. Use IAM users or roles instead.

checks.title: IAM users have MFA enabled
checks.remediation: Enable MFA for all IAM users: AWS Console → IAM → Users → Select user → Security Credentials → Assign MFA device.

checks.title: No unused IAM credentials
checks.reason: 19 users to review for unused credentials
checks.remediation: Disable or remove unused IAM credentials. Run: aws iam generate-credential-report and review users with no recent activity.

checks.title: Strong password policy
checks.reason: Password policy configuration could not be verified
checks.remediation: Set strong password policy: AWS Console → IAM → Account Settings → Password Policy. Require 14+ chars, uppercase, lowercase, numbers, symbols.

checks.title: No inline IAM policies
checks.remediation: Move inline policies to managed policies. Use aws iam put-user-policy to create managed policies and attach them.

checks.title: No overly permissive policies
checks.remediation: Review and restrict IAM policies with Action: '*' and Resource: '*'. Apply least privilege principle.

checks.title: S3 buckets not publicly accessible
checks.reason: 27 buckets to review for public access
checks.remediation: Block public access: AWS Console → S3 → Select bucket → Permissions → Block Public Access → Enable all settings.

checks.title: S3 bucket encryption enabled
checks.remediation: Enable default encryption: AWS Console → S3 → Bucket → Properties → Default Encryption → Enable with AES-256 or AWS KMS.

checks.title: S3 bucket logging enabled
checks.remediation: Enable access logging: AWS Console → S3 → Bucket → Properties → Server Access Logging → Enable with target bucket.

checks.title: S3 bucket versioning enabled
checks.remediation: Enable versioning: AWS Console → S3 → Bucket → Properties → Bucket Versioning → Enable.

checks.title: S3 bucket enforces SSL
checks.remediation: Add bucket policy condition: 'aws:SecureTransport': 'false' → Effect: Deny to enforce HTTPS.

checks.title: CloudTrail enabled in all regions
checks.remediation: Create a multi-region trail: AWS Console → CloudTrail → Create Trail → Apply to all regions → Enable.

checks.title: CloudTrail log file validation
checks.remediation: Enable log file validation: AWS Console → CloudTrail → Trail → Edit → Enable log file validation.

checks.title: GuardDuty enabled
checks.reason: GuardDuty is not enabled in any region
checks.remediation: Enable GuardDuty: AWS Console → GuardDuty → Get Started → Enable GuardDuty.

checks.title: No unrestricted SSH access
checks.reason: No security groups allow unrestricted SSH access
checks.remediation: Restrict SSH access to specific IPs: AWS Console → EC2 → Security Groups → Edit Inbound Rules → Change 0.0.0.0/0 to specific CIDR.

checks.title: No unrestricted RDP access
checks.reason: 4 security group(s) allow unrestricted RDP access
checks.remediation: Restrict RDP access to specific IPs. Consider using AWS Systems Manager Session Manager instead of direct RDP.

checks.title: No unrestricted ingress on sensitive ports
checks.reason: 1 security group(s) allow unrestricted database access
checks.remediation: Restrict database port access to application security groups only. Never expose databases to the internet.

checks.title: Default security group restricts all traffic
checks.remediation: Remove all inbound and outbound rules from the default security group. Create custom security groups instead.

checks.title: VPC flow logs enabled
checks.reason: 22 VPC(s) to verify flow log configuration
checks.remediation: Enable VPC Flow Logs: AWS Console → VPC → Select VPC → Flow Logs → Create flow log with CloudWatch or S3 destination.

checks.title: EC2 instances without public IPs
checks.remediation: Remove public IPs from instances that don't need internet access. Use NAT Gateway or VPC endpoints for outbound traffic.

checks.title: EC2 uses IMDSv2
checks.remediation: Enable IMDSv2: aws ec2 modify-instance-metadata-options --instance-id <id> --http-tokens required.

checks.title: EBS volumes encrypted
checks.remediation: Enable EBS encryption by default: AWS Console → EC2 → Settings → EBS Encryption → Always encrypt new EBS volumes.

checks.reason: Root account MFA is NOT enabled
checks.remediation: Enable MFA on the root account: AWS Console → IAM → Dashboard → Activate MFA on root account. Use a hardware MFA device for maximum security.

checks.title: IAM users have MFA enabled
checks.remediation: Enable MFA for all IAM users: AWS Console → IAM → Users → Select user → Security Credentials → Assign MFA device.

checks.reason: Root account MFA is NOT enabled
checks.remediation: Enable MFA on the root account: AWS Console → IAM → Dashboard → Activate MFA on root account. Use a hardware MFA device for maximum security.

checks.title: No root account access keys
checks.remediation: Delete root account access keys: AWS Console → IAM → Security Credentials → Delete access keys. Use IAM users or roles instead.

checks.title: IAM users have MFA enabled
checks.remediation: Enable MFA for all IAM users: AWS Console → IAM → Users → Select user → Security Credentials → Assign MFA device.

checks.title: No overly permissive policies
checks.remediation: Review and restrict IAM policies with Action: '*' and Resource: '*'. Apply least privilege principle.

checks.title: Strong password policy
checks.reason: Password policy configuration could not be verified
checks.remediation: Set strong password policy: AWS Console → IAM → Account Settings → Password Policy. Require 14+ chars, uppercase, lowercase, numbers, symbols.

checks.title: CloudTrail enabled in all regions
checks.remediation: Create a multi-region trail: AWS Console → CloudTrail → Create Trail → Apply to all regions → Enable.

checks.title: GuardDuty enabled
checks.reason: GuardDuty is not enabled in any region
checks.remediation: Enable GuardDuty: AWS Console → GuardDuty → Get Started → Enable GuardDuty.

checks.title: VPC flow logs enabled
checks.reason: 22 VPC(s) to verify flow log configuration
checks.remediation: Enable VPC Flow Logs: AWS Console → VPC → Select VPC → Flow Logs → Create flow log with CloudWatch or S3 destination.

checks.title: CloudTrail log file validation
checks.remediation: Enable log file validation: AWS Console → CloudTrail → Trail → Edit → Enable log file validation.

checks.title: No unrestricted SSH access
checks.reason: No security groups allow unrestricted SSH access
checks.remediation: Restrict SSH access to specific IPs: AWS Console → EC2 → Security Groups → Edit Inbound Rules → Change 0.0.0.0/0 to specific CIDR.

checks.title: No unrestricted RDP access
checks.reason: 4 security group(s) allow unrestricted RDP access
checks.remediation: Restrict RDP access to specific IPs. Consider using AWS Systems Manager Session Manager instead of direct RDP.

checks.title: No unrestricted ingress on sensitive ports
checks.reason: 1 security group(s) allow unrestricted database access
checks.remediation: Restrict database port access to application security groups only. Never expose databases to the internet.

checks.title: Default security group restricts all traffic
checks.remediation: Remove all inbound and outbound rules from the default security group. Create custom security groups instead.

checks.title: EC2 instances without public IPs
checks.remediation: Remove public IPs from instances that don't need internet access. Use NAT Gateway or VPC endpoints for outbound traffic.

checks.title: EC2 uses IMDSv2
checks.remediation: Enable IMDSv2: aws ec2 modify-instance-metadata-options --instance-id <id> --http-tokens required.

checks.title: S3 bucket encryption enabled
checks.remediation: Enable default encryption: AWS Console → S3 → Bucket → Properties → Default Encryption → Enable with AES-256 or AWS KMS.

checks.title: S3 buckets not publicly accessible
checks.reason: 27 buckets to review for public access
checks.remediation: Block public access: AWS Console → S3 → Select bucket → Permissions → Block Public Access → Enable all settings.

checks.title: S3 bucket enforces SSL
checks.remediation: Add bucket policy condition: 'aws:SecureTransport': 'false' → Effect: Deny to enforce HTTPS.

checks.title: RDS encryption at rest enabled
checks.reason: All RDS instances have encryption enabled
checks.remediation: Enable encryption at rest. Note: Existing unencrypted instances must be migrated (snapshot → encrypt → restore).

checks.title: RDS instances not publicly accessible
checks.remediation: Disable public access: AWS Console → RDS → Modify Instance → Connectivity → Public Access → No.

checks.title: EBS volumes encrypted
checks.remediation: Enable EBS encryption by default: AWS Console → EC2 → Settings → EBS Encryption → Always encrypt new EBS volumes.

checks.title: EBS snapshots encrypted
checks.remediation: Enable encryption for new snapshots. Copy unencrypted snapshots with encryption enabled.

checks.title: EBS snapshots not public
checks.remediation: Modify snapshot permissions: AWS Console → EC2 → Snapshots → Modify Permissions → Make Private.

checks.title: GuardDuty enabled
checks.reason: GuardDuty is not enabled in any region
checks.remediation: Enable GuardDuty: AWS Console → GuardDuty → Get Started → Enable GuardDuty.

checks.title: CloudTrail enabled in all regions
checks.remediation: Create a multi-region trail: AWS Console → CloudTrail → Create Trail → Apply to all regions → Enable.

ai_recommendations.title: Address Critical Security Issues Immediately
ai_recommendations.description: Found 27 critical compliance violation(s) that require immediate attention.
items: Root account MFA enabled — Root account MFA is NOT enabled
No unrestricted RDP access — 4 security group(s) allow unrestricted RDP access
No unrestricted ingress on sensitive ports — 1 security group(s) allow unrestricted database access
Root account MFA enabled — Root account MFA is NOT enabled
No unrestricted RDP access — 4 security group(s) allow unrestricted RDP access
ai_recommendations.impact: These issues expose your infrastructure to significant security risks including unauthorized access and data breaches.

ai_recommendations.title: General Security Hardening
ai_recommendations.description: Implement security best practices to improve overall compliance posture.
items: Enable encryption at rest for all data stores (S3, RDS, EBS)
Enforce MFA for all IAM users with console access
Restrict security group rules to specific CIDRs
Enable CloudTrail and GuardDuty in all regions
Enable VPC Flow Logs for network monitoring
ai_recommendations.impact: These measures significantly reduce your attack surface and improve compliance scores.
